events.title: US Implements Blockade of Iranian Ports After Talks Collapse
events.desc: The US military announced it would commence a blockade of all maritime traffic entering and exiting Iranian ports and coastal areas. This action follows the failure of weekend talks in Islamabad aimed at ending the war with Iran, jeopardizing a fragile two-week ceasefire. The US President cited Iran's handling of the Strait of Hormuz as a reason for the blockade.

events.title: Iran Slams US Blockade Threat as 'Piracy'
events.desc: Iran's military has declared that a US threat to impose a naval blockade on the Strait of Hormuz and Iranian ports would be illegal and constitute 'piracy.' The Iranian military warned that no Gulf ports would remain safe if its own were threatened, emphasizing the illegality of US restrictions on maritime navigation.

events.title: UK and France to Co-Host Talks on Defensive Naval Mission for Hormuz
events.desc: Britain and France will co-host talks this week to discuss restoring freedom of navigation in the Strait of Hormuz, including a potential strictly defensive naval mission. The talks aim to bring together nations willing to join a multinational mission for safe passage through the strait.

events.title: Israeli Airstrike Kills Three Palestinians in Gaza Amid Ceasefire Talks
events.desc: An Israeli airstrike killed at least three Palestinians in the Gaza Strip while mediators were meeting with Hamas leaders to solidify a US-brokered ceasefire deal. The strike hit a group of men outside a school in Deir al-Balah, with the Israeli military yet to comment.

events.title: Iranian Military Strikes Prince Sultan Air Base, Damaging Aircraft
events.desc: Iranian drones and missiles struck Prince Sultan Air Base in Saudi Arabia, destroying an E-3 Sentry and damaging multiple KC-135 tankers. This is part of a broader Iranian counterair campaign waged through asymmetric means against US and Israeli forces.
events.location: Prince Sultan Air Base, Saudi Arabia

events.title: US Deploys Mines Over Iranian Village, Causing Fatalities
events.desc: Evidence suggests the US deployed the Gator Scatterable Mine system over the village of Kafari in southern Iran, resulting in multiple fatalities according to Iranian media. Experts identified the munitions as likely US-made Gator anti-tank mines.

events.title: Two Waves of Bombing Strike IRGC Compound and Adjacent School in Minab
events.desc: New videos confirm two waves of strikes hit an IRGC compound and an adjacent school in Minab, Iran. Released by Iran's Ministry of Foreign Affairs, the videos show multiple missiles impacting the complex, providing further details on the incident.

events.title: Iranian Government Imposes Internet Blackout Amid Strikes
events.desc: During recent strikes on military installations, the Iranian government deliberately dismantled communication infrastructure and halted internet traffic, causing a 98% collapse in connectivity. This blackout was imposed on the Iranian populace, distinct from the external attacks on military targets.

events.title: Israel Unleashes Deadly Blitz on Lebanon Hours After US-Iran Ceasefire
events.desc: Hours after a US-Iran ceasefire was announced, Israel launched a large wave of strikes across Lebanon, hitting Beirut's southern suburbs, southern Lebanon, and the eastern Bekaa Valley. This action resulted in at least 182 deaths and 890 wounded, according to the Lebanese health ministry, contradicting the ceasefire's intent.

events.title: US-Iran Talks in Islamabad Fail to Yield Deal, Ceasefire in Jeopardy
events.desc: High-level talks between the US and Iran in Islamabad collapsed over the weekend, failing to secure a comprehensive deal to end the ongoing war. This failure jeopardizes a fragile two-week ceasefire and has led to the US initiating a naval blockade of Iranian ports.

events.title: Hungarian Government Passwords Exposed Online, Affecting Multiple Ministries
events.desc: Approximately 800 Hungarian government email addresses and their associated passwords have been leaked online. A Bellingcat analysis indicates that 12 out of 13 ministries were affected, potentially exposing sensitive and classified information due to basic security vulnerabilities.

events.title: Tear Gas Fired at Indian Workers Protesting Rising Living Costs
events.desc: Factory workers in a suburb of India's capital protested against rising living costs, exacerbated by the ongoing Iran war. Police responded by firing tear gas at the demonstrators.

events.title: Lebanon and Israel Officials to Meet in US Amidst Ceasefire Tensions
events.desc: Officials from Lebanon and Israel are scheduled to meet in the United States for talks. This meeting follows a period of heightened tensions, including Israeli strikes in Lebanon after a US-Iran ceasefire announcement, which Lebanon argues should have included it.

events.title: Palestinian Shot Dead During Israeli Settler Attack in West Bank
events.desc: A Palestinian was killed during an attack by Israeli settlers on a village in the occupied West Bank. This incident occurred amidst warnings from ex-security chiefs about 'government-sponsored Jewish terrorism' escalating.

events.title: Iran Claims US Blockade of Hormuz 'Amounts to Piracy'
events.desc: Iran's army has stated that US plans to blockade the Strait of Hormuz and Iranian ports are illegal and 'amount to piracy.' The military warned that restricting maritime navigation in international waters is an unlawful act, and no ports in the Gulf would be safe if Iran's were threatened.
